user_query:
I’m a Polish businesswoman screening for expansion-heavy companies. Using the provided company list and keyword set, please compile and critique expansion/investment signals from official press releases.

Inputs (under input/):
- input/companies.csv (columns: company,domain) — the official corporate domains to constrain your searches.
- input/keywords.yaml (YAML list under "keywords") — count these case-insensitive, whole-word terms in each press release’s text.

Task:
1) For each company in input/companies.csv, use a search engine to find official press/news/IR pages on that company’s domain (e.g., using site:<domain> queries). Identify up to 5 press releases published in calendar year 2023 that contain at least one keyword from input/keywords.yaml in the page text. If fewer than 3 such pages can be found for 2023, extend the search window to 2022–2024 until you have up to 5 total, and record the year detected for each page. Only use pages on the specified official domains; skip paywalled or non-official sources.
2) Download each matched press release page as HTML and extract visible text (exclude scripts/styles). Save both artifacts using a consistent base name per page.
3) Compute per-page keyword counts and per-company aggregates. Use case-insensitive whole-word matching for each keyword in input/keywords.yaml.
4) Produce a short, structured memo that judges which companies exhibit stronger expansion signals based on a transparent rule: flag a company as "Aggressive expansion signal" if (a) it has at least 3 matched releases and (b) its average total keyword hits per matched release is more than 1.5× the median of the per-company averages. Otherwise label it "Neutral". Keep the memo factual and tied to the computed numbers.

Required outputs (create these exact paths):
- output/raw_html/<company_slug>/<basename>.html — raw HTML for each page (company_slug = lowercase company with spaces replaced by hyphens; basename may use YYYYMMDD_title or similar).
- output/text/<company_slug>/<basename>.txt — extracted visible text for each downloaded page.
- output/metadata/press_releases.jsonl — one JSON object per line with fields: company, domain, url, page_title, published_date (YYYY-MM-DD or null), year, company_slug, basename, keyword_hits (object of keyword->count), total_keyword_hits (integer).
- output/summary/keyword_stats.csv — columns: company, num_releases, total_keyword_hits, avg_hits_per_release, median_avg_hits_across_companies, ratio_vs_median, signal ("Aggressive expansion signal" or "Neutral"). Include one row per company and a final row with company="ALL" summarizing totals/overall average (leave ratio_vs_median and signal blank for the ALL row).
- output/report/expansion_signals_memo.md — max 400 words; include: (a) brief method summary (how pages were found and filtered), (b) a compact table or bullet list of per-company metrics (num_releases, avg_hits, ratio_vs_median, signal), and (c) a judgement section naming any flagged companies with a 1–2 sentence business rationale tied to the observed counts.
- output/logs/search_log.jsonl — for reproducibility, append one line per executed query with fields: company, domain, engine, query, inspected_urls (array, up to 10), selected_urls (array of the URLs you chose from that query).

Constraints & notes:
- The search engine step is mandatory and must be logged in output/logs/search_log.jsonl. Do not hardcode URLs; discover them via queries restricted to the provided domains.
- The download-and-extract step is mandatory; compute counts from the extracted text files, not from HTML directly.
- Whole-word keyword matching should be case-insensitive and applied to the extracted text.
- If you cannot reach 3 matched 2023 pages for a company even after extending to 2022–2024, proceed with what you found and note this in the memo.

Deliver back all specified files under the output/ directory. The goal is a reproducible, numbers-driven judgement on which of these official sources signal comparatively stronger expansion. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
